title: How will enrolling in Marketplace coverage affect me?
meta-title: How will enrolling in Marketplace coverage affect me?
meta-description: American Indians and Alaska Natives who enroll in Medicaid, the Children’s Health Insurance Program (CHIP), or the Marketplace can get or continue to get services from the Indian Health Service, tribal health programs, or urban Indian health programs.
bite: American Indians and Alaska Natives who enroll in Medicaid, the Children’s Health Insurance Program (CHIP), or the Marketplace can get or continue to get services from the Indian Health Service, tribal health programs, or urban Indian health programs.
page_name: How will enrolling in Marketplace coverage affect me?
page_topic: How will enrolling in Marketplace coverage affect me?
excerpt: American Indians and Alaska Natives may qualify for special benefits through the Marketplace. If you or anyone else on your application is American Indian or Alaska Native, select “Yes” when you’re asked this question.
content:
American Indians and Alaska Natives may qualify for special benefits through the Marketplace. If you or anyone else on your application is American Indian or Alaska Native, select “Yes” when you’re asked this question.

American Indians and Alaska Natives who enroll in Medicaid, the Children’s Health Insurance Program (CHIP), or the Marketplace can get or continue to get services from the Indian Health Service, tribal health programs, or urban Indian health programs. They also may not have to pay cost sharing and may get monthly Special Enrollment Periods.
